regime_read: ES & NQ moving together — broad agreement, more DURABLE directional signal. Trust the direction more.
narrative:
Macro backdrop —
[2026-06-29] US economic state (FRED):
  Growth: real GDP 2.1% (q/q ann)
  Inflation: CPI 3.9% YoY | core CPI 2.58% | core PCE 3.14% (Fed target 2%)
  Labor: unemployment 4.3%
  Rates: fed funds 3.63% | 10Y 4.4% | 10Y-2Y 0.31% (NORMAL)
  Consumer: UMich sentiment 44.8 | VIX 18.89

---
PRE-US-OPEN BRIEFING — ES/NQ pair, as of 2026-06-29 ~12:22 UTC.

1) RECENT MOVE-BY-MOVE READ. The tape is recovering off a rough week. NQ closed the week down ~3.0% and ES down ~1.0%, with both having tagged their prior-week lows (NQ 29,160 / ES 7,357.25) — that drawdown is consistent with the geopolitical risk-off cluster dominating the headline tape: US strikes on Iran into a second day, Iran proposing a regional security framework after attacks on Bahrain/Kuwait, and Ukrainian drone strikes igniting a major Russian oil refinery. That's an oil-supply/risk-premium backdrop that pressured high-beta tech harder than the broad index — hence NQ's deeper weekly bleed vs ES. Today, though, both are bouncing: ES +0.88% and NQ +1.21% on the session, with each trading back above its own session VWAP (ES last 7,466.75 vs VWAP 7,454.85; NQ last 29,724.75 vs VWAP 29,652.98). So the intraday read is a relief/stabilization bounce off the weekly lows, with NQ leading the recovery in percentage terms — typical when an oversold growth complex snaps back. RSI sits neutral-mid on both (ES 52.9, NQ 52.0): bounce, not yet momentum. Both remain well above the 200-day (ES +7.3%, NQ +13.8%) — the larger uptrend structure is intact; this is a pullback-within-uptrend, not a regime break.

2) VOLUME-PROFILE READ. NQ: 5-day POC at 29,667, value area 29,362–29,845, with a stacked high-volume node shelf (29,667 / 29,768 / 29,641 / 29,590 / 29,565). Price (29,725) is sitting just above POC and inside the upper half of the value area — constructive. The accumulation zone is that 29,565–29,768 shelf; that's where supply/demand balanced over the week and where dip-buyers have been engaging. The line in the sand is the lower value-area edge near 29,362, which lines up almost exactly with the prior-week low (29,160) just below — lose value-area-low and the bounce thesis is in question. ES: POC 7,425, value area 7,392–7,480, HVN cluster 7,418–7,451; price (7,467) is in the upper value area, above POC — same constructive posture. ES line in the sand: value-area-low ~7,392 / prior-week-low 7,357. CAVEAT: this is volume-AT-price (where trade happened), NOT order-flow delta — I cannot see buy/sell aggression, so treat HVNs as magnets/structure, not proof of who's in control.

3) NQ/ES LEAD-LAG REGIME. Classification: BREADTH. Contemporaneous correlation is high (0.938), lead-lag is essentially flat (best lag corr only 0.058, leader = NONE), and divergence = false. ES and NQ are moving as one body — no concentration spike, no rotation warning. Implication: the direction (currently up off the lows) is MORE durable / more trustworthy than in a concentration or divergent tape, so the read justifies normal-to-slightly-fuller trust in the directional signal. It does NOT by itself justify oversized risk — breadth tells you to trust the SIGN, not to lever up. (Honest note: my own regime-accuracy on the scorecard is only 0.33 over n=8 — calibrating — so this regime label is an input, not gospel.)

4) EVENT-CALENDAR AWARENESS. Heavy near-term docket: PCE (Fed's preferred inflation) tomorrow 2026-06-30 at 8:30 ET — reduce size into that window; ISM Manufacturing 07-01 10:00 ET — trim into the print; Nonfarm Payrolls 07-03 8:30 ET — high-impact, widen stops / size down; CPI 07-12. Note these (ex-FOMC) are cadence ESTIMATES, not verified release dates, so confirm against the official calendar — but the practical takeaway holds: today/this session you are trading INTO a PCE print tomorrow morning. Any long held overnight carries event risk. Size down into the 06-30 window regardless of conviction.

5) SYNTHESIS. Meta-regime: pullback-within-uptrend + BREADTH co-movement + a relief bounce off prior-week lows, set against a still-hot geopolitical/oil headline tape and a PCE print one day out. The constructive evidence (both back above VWAP, both above POC and in upper value area, both far above 200DMA, clean high correlation with no divergence) outweighs the bearish residue (negative weekly returns, neutral RSI, unresolved geopolitical risk). That nets to a modest LONG / long-the-dip-continuation bias, ~58% — NARROWED but deliberately not high-conviction because: news is NOT clean (active military escalation = tail risk that can gap the tape regardless of structure), and a major inflation print sits 1 day out. Confidence is capped at 58 for exactly that reason — high confidence requires clear news AND clean lead-lag; I only have the latter.

BIAS: LONG (dip-buy continuation), ~58%, INVALIDATED on a sustained break below NQ 29,360 (value-area-low; below that, next reference is the 29,160 prior-week low and the thesis flips to defensive). For ES the analogous invalidation is ~7,392 / 7,357. This is intelligence and a structured read of the system's real signals — NOT a validated edge. My briefing scorecard is still calibrating (n=8); treat the probability as directional intuition with documented reasoning, not a proven win-rate. Also per the system note: this is ES/NQ research only — no execution path here.
